question: What did we conclude was causing the DataTable component to re-render on every keystroke in the search input?
turns: The DataTable component in our admin dashboard re-renders on every keystroke in the search box, even when the table data hasn't changed. Using React 18.2, profiler shows 40-60ms paint per re-render causing visible lag.
Verified the parent SearchPage is wrapped in React.memo. Also confirmed the data prop — a stable array from a Redux selector — is not changing reference between renders.

Found it: we're passing style={{ overflow: 'auto', position: 'relative' }} as an inline prop to DataTable. Every render of SearchPage creates a new object literal so DataTable's props.style reference changes every time, bypassing the memo check.

Moved the style object to a module-level constant outside the component. Re-renders stopped entirely. Root cause: inline object literal prop creating a new reference on each parent render, defeating React.memo.
